summary: Do not repeatedly resume if getMore receives the same error
description: See DRIVERS-907 for details.

summary: Tests drivers raise error if hint specified for unacknowledged delete using OP_MSG or OP_DELETE
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    I would like to include in the CRUD spec that the driver should raise an error for an unacknowledged delete operations with a hint option. In addition, I would like to add tests for this functionality, as well as the related update functionality described in DRIVERS-790 (no tests were added for that change).
    See DRIVERS-970 for updated details.

summary: Ensure that the WriteConcernError "errInfo" object is propagated
description: See DRIVERS-967 for details.

summary: Remove ElectionInProgress (216) from ResumableChangeStreamError
GridFS index checking should support indexes created in the shell
description: See DRIVERS-789 for details.

summary: [FLE] Add a corpus test that exhaustively enumerates all ways to encrypt all BSON value types
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    A new spec "corpus" test was added, where each driver constructs a document with encryption along all of these axes, producing a document like this, which we can make assertions on (deterministic explicit matches automatic, random differs, all can be explicitly decrypted, etc.).

Creating tickets only for C#, Go, Java and Python as implementation of the FLE spec is in progress and rest of the drivers have not started. 
    See DRIVERS-686 for updated details.

summary: allowDiskUse option for find should be documented as only being supported in 4.4+
description: See DRIVERS-1193 for updated details.

summary: Do not add the RetryableWriteError label to errors that occur during a write within a transaction (excepting commitTransaction and abortTransaction)
description: The corresponding spec change clarifies the conditions under which the driver should add the RetryableWritesError label to errors within a transaction, and adds some transaction tests to ensure that the driver does not add the label to errors that occur on writes within a transaction.

summary: Aggregate operation should implement Explainable
description:
The [{{explain}}|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/explain/] command supports the [{{aggregate}}|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/aggregate/] command, which means our Aggregate operation can implement the Explainable interface as an alternative to using the {{explain}} command option.

When implementing this, we should confirm whether {{explain}} has always supported {{aggregate}} or if it was introduced in some later server version.

summary: Database::command() should not inherit read preference
description: [{{Database::command()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/blob/81a57c0d1fca902ae74eb4ae24f4ca23dbb08782/src/Database.php#L250] presently inherits the Database's read preference. This conflicts with [Use of read preferences with commands|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-selection/server-selection.rst#use-of-read-preferences-with-commands] from the Server Selection spec.

summary: Better document mongodb extension installation steps
description:
Installation of the {{mongodb}} extension requires _libssl-dev_ and _pkg-config_ installed. This requirement is only documented within the [php.net module page|https://www.php.net/manual/en/mongodb.installation.php], which our documentation links to. The [php.net requirements|https://www.php.net/manual/en/mongodb.requirements.php] page also appears to show more information than our docs.

We should consider paraphasing the {{mongodb}} extension installation steps in our installation docs, which should also clearly indicate the requirements.

summary: Link to CRUD tutorial from entry page of library manual
description:
The [CRUD operations tutorial|https://docs.mongodb.com/php-library/current/tutorial/crud/] contains content that is several years old and derived from the Ruby docs at the time of writing.

After a Product Report review [~jmikola] suggested that we'd do well to revisit the entry page for the library manual.

summary: Treat CursorNotFound as a resumable change stream error
description: See DRIVERS-1308 for updated details.

summary: Consult NamespaceNotFound error code in DropCollection
description: DropCollection was never updated to consider the NamespaceNotFound(26) error code, which is available in MongoDB 3.2 (since [1725d76|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo/commit/1725d76f448323a2bbaa11ffd37fd7b10cd6a64b] for SERVER-17573).

summary: GridFS methods should throw if stream_copy_to_stream() fails
description:
The following GridFS methods use [{{stream_copy_to_stream()}}|https://php.net/stream_copy_to_stream]:

 * downloadToStream
 * downloadToStreamByName
 * uploadFromStream

These methods currently throw exceptions if the file cannot be opened or a driver exception is encountered, but they do not check that {{stream_copy_to_stream()}} succeeds.

Users may already be aware of stream IO errors since our StreamWrapper may emit an {{E_USER_WARNING}} with an internal exception message, but we would do well to throw an exception if {{stream_copy_to_stream()}} returns boolean {{false}}.

summary: Create Evergreen matrix for various test configurations
GridFS stream close handler may error during shutdown
description:
This came up during a [code review|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/pull/527/files#r182849516] for PHPLIB-233:

{code:php}
<?php

require 'vendor/autoload.php';

$bucket = (new MongoDB\Client)->test->selectGridFSBucket();

$stream = $bucket->openUploadStream('filename');
{code}

The following produces the following error when run as a CLI script:

{noformat}
Warning: hash_final(): supplied resource is not a valid Hash Context resource in /home/jmikola/workspace/mongodb/phplib/src/GridFS/WritableStream.php on line 222

Fatal error: Uncaught Error: Class 'MongoDB\Operation\FindOne' not found in /home/jmikola/workspace/mongodb/phplib/src/Collection.php:594
Stack trace:
#0 /home/jmikola/workspace/mongodb/phplib/src/GridFS/CollectionWrapper.php(333): MongoDB\Collection->findOne(Array, Array)
#1 /home/jmikola/workspace/mongodb/phplib/src/GridFS/CollectionWrapper.php(317): MongoDB\GridFS\CollectionWrapper->isFilesCollectionEmpty()
#2 /home/jmikola/workspace/mongodb/phplib/src/GridFS/CollectionWrapper.php(254): MongoDB\GridFS\CollectionWrapper->ensureIndexes()
#3 /home/jmikola/workspace/mongodb/phplib/src/GridFS/WritableStream.php(229): MongoDB\GridFS\CollectionWrapper->insertFile(Array)
#4 /home/jmikola/workspace/mongodb/phplib/src/GridFS/WritableStream.php(137): MongoDB\GridFS\WritableStream->fileCollectionInsert()
#5 /home/jmikola/workspace/mongodb/phplib/src/GridFS/StreamWrapper.php(73): MongoDB\GridFS\WritableStream->close()
#6 [internal function]: MongoDB\GridFS\StreamWrapper->stream_close()
#7 {main}
  thrown in /home/jmikola/workspace/mongodb/phplib/src/Collection.php on line 594
{noformat}

Running this script through PHP 7.2 produces the following errors:

 * The MD5 hash context has already been freed before we finalize it
 * The {{MongoDB\Operation\FindOne}} class cannot be autoloaded at this point

On PHP 5.6, only the autoloading error appears (with a reference to {{spl_autoload()}} being unable to load the class).

I believe the commonality in both of these errors is that we're running logic after the request has ended. As the last reference to the {{$stream}} is removed, its close handler is invoked. We should investigate whether there is a workaround for this short of advising users to manually close any streams defined in a global context.

summary: Add MongoDB version axis to Evergreen matrix
description:
Test against all supported server versions and ensure the build is stable:
* 3.2
* 3.4
* 3.6
* 4.0
* 4.2
* 4.4
* 4.5

summary: Add PHP runtime axis to Evergreen matrix
description:
Tested PHP versions:
* 7.0
* 7.1
* 7.2
* 7.3

# PHP 7.4 and 8.0 (in alpha stage) are not available yet.

summary: CreateIndexes should not specify "ns" for index specifications
description:
CreateIndexes currently specifies the namespace as an "ns" field in the index specification document for {{createIndexes}} commands. While that was required for legacy index creation (i.e. {{system.indexes}} insertions) it has never been applicable to the [createIndexes|https://docs.mongodb.com/v2.6/reference/command/createIndexes/] command. The server does not appear to apply strict validation and raise errors for unsupported index options; however, we'd do well to fix this in case a future version starts doing so.

This code was introduced prior to 1.0 in [4de66e3|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/commit/4de66e39d72c509f87e77d78a5de11f45a18b0b9#diff-810cdcfdd8a6b9e1fc0d1e96d7786874R311] and was preserved when porting Collection methods to operation classes in [5b7bb9a|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/commit/5b7bb9a43ba24409e10e884606fa5427d2754799].

I discovered this while working on the unified test runner (PHPLIB-493), since it is more strict about matching command documents.

summary: Use get_debug_type for reporting class and primitive type info
Update release checklist to include step to file docs ticket outlining compatibility changes/lack thereof
description:
Acceptance Criteria: 
* (x) Include a step in the checklist to file a ticket for Docs specifically highlighting changes in compatibility or the lack thereof (driver version to server version AND driver version to language version)

summary: Clean up cursor usages to account for iterator changes
description:
Starting with ext-mongodb 1.9.0, {{MongoDB\Driver\Cursor}} implements the {{Iterator}} interface, which allows us to simplify some of the logic around cursors.

In many cases, we wrap the original cursor in a {{IteratorIterator}} to call the typical iterator methods (e.g. {{rewind}}). This is no longer necessary, as it just forwards the logic to the cursor and calls the corresponding method there.

Note that PHPLIB 1.8.0 did not bump the PHPC dependency in composer.json. This is because it is compatible with both PHPC 1.8.0 and PHPC 1.9.0. Not requiring the newer version helps reduce friction during this upgrade process, hopefully speeding up adoption of PHP 8 compatible releases. PHPLIB 1.9.0 will have to bump its PHPC dependency in order to be able to make these changes to cursor iteration.

summary: CachingIterator uses wrong check for validity
description: In {{storeCurrentItem}}, the {{CachingIterator}} class checks the nested iterator for validity by checking that the key is not {{null}} instead of using the {{valid()}} method of the nested iterator. In combination with PHPC-1748, this causes invalid positions to be treated as valid.

summary: Intentionally failing tests for unified test runner are never executed
description:
In [4608a73|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/commit/4608a734993be0b0aa1e0e838fa52e4caa9bfff2#diff-58846c56a14f8262956f22c9563867bcdc800ce8159e1037b480ad5b63889b72R161], the {{UnifiedSpecTest::testFailingTests}} method calls a non-existent "testCase" method. This error was ignored because the surrounding try/catch uses Throwable instead of Exception.

Changing the catch condition highlights the issue. When fixing this, we should confirm that all of the "valid-fail" test cases actually fail with exceptions and none create PHP errors.

summary: Support Azure and GCP keystores in FLE
description: See DRIVERS-812 for updated details.

summary: Change estimatedDocumentCount() to use the $collStats Agg Stage Instead of Count Command
Versioned MongoDB API for Drivers
description: See DRIVERS-996 for updated details.

summary: Unified test runner changes for Atlas testing
Workarounds for killAllSessions in unified test runner
Workload executor for Atlas testing
Resync transaction spec tests for validating unpinning after abort
description:
Resync transactions spec tests with https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/ec326862be443d11f0233cee2de32a95751144df

Depends on PHPC-1819, which will pull in necessary changes from CDRIVER-3949.

summary: Clarify behavior for runOn.minServerVersion for latest servers
Replace usages of 'acceptAPIVersion2' with 'acceptApiVersion2'
Ensure isClientError assertions are handled
description: ExpectedError parses {{isClientError}} upon construction, but it is never used for an assertion. This was likely missed in the original unified test runner implementation (PHPLIB-493).

summary: ListIndexes should use CommandException for catching missing namespace and database errors
description: ListIndexes catches a {{MongoDB\Driver\Exception\RuntimeException}} to check error codes and suppress missing namespace/database errors. At the very least it should use ServerException (first class where codes are guaranteed to originate from the server), but likely CommandException for consistency with other classes (e.g. DropCollection).

summary: Require passing Stable API options to getMore and transaction-continuing commands
Topology match in RunOnRequirement::isSatisfied may preempt later checks
description:
[d1b18dd|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/commit/d1b18dd51dad81eea60ab7b71a7bcc0c0190845e] for PHPLIB-622 introduced server parameter checks to RunOnRequirement::isSatisfied; however, it did not remove earlier {{return true}} cases from topology checks. This means that a topology match could prevent the server parameter check from occurring at all.

If not addressed, this could also affect {{auth}} and {{serverless}} checks that will be added by PHPLIB-669 and PHPLIB-670, respectively.

summary: Fix syntax error in example code
description:
Syntax error in red circle

document：https://docs.mongodb.com/php-library/master/tutorial/collation/

summary: Allow hint for unacknowledged writes using OP_MSG when supported by the server
description:
*Downstream changes*: Drivers should no longer prohibit using {{hint}} with an unacknowledged write concern for update, delete, and findAndModify commands (issued via OP_MSG) and related helpers (e.g. UpdateOne, bulkWrite, findOneAndDelete) if {{hint}} is known to be supported by the server executing the operation (i.e. 4.2+ for update commands, 4.4+ for delete and findAndModify). Drivers should sync unified CRUD tests with [mongodb/specifications@59a5dad|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/59a5dadb2eeab8bba5a7d9a9acd47aa8c1d594cf]
----
This reverts DRIVERS-991, which instructed drivers to raise a client-side error when the {{hint}} option is provided with an unacknowledged write issued against any server version.

When the the server is known to support {{hint}} (i.e. 4.2+ for update, 4.4+ for delete), drivers should allow the option to be specified. They should only report an error for earlier server versions where the server is known to *not* support the option and an error would otherwise not be returned because of the nature of unacknowledged writes.

Previous arguments relating {{hint}} to existing treatment of {{arrayFilters}} and {{collation}} aren't relevant, because their documentation refers to "unacknowledged writes using OP_UPDATE" (where the option cannot be expressed). Those options don't refer to OP_MSG at all, because they were both supported by all server versions that support OP_MSG. {{hint}} can retain the language about OP_UPDATE, but its OP_MSG language should be revised to only mandate an error on unsupported server versions.

Lastly, the following [Q&A|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/crud/crud.rst#q-a] should be revised:
{quote}Q: Why are client-side errors raised when options are provided for unacknowledged write operations, even on server versions that support those options?
 Even if the server supports an option, that option could be incorrectly formatted or contain outdated information (e.g. a "hint" option referencing an index that no longer exists). This is a problem during unacknowledged writes because the server cannot return an error response to alert the client of an incorrect option. By always raising a client-side error when options are specified for unacknowledged writes, users are prevented from performing operations with options that do not work.
{quote}
This is a poor argument as incorrectly formatted options can just as easily apply to the filter or update/replacement arguments (e.g. using an invalid query operator). It's one thing to raise a client-error when the driver knows for certain that a server option is not supported, but the current strategy is presumptive.

We should also update the language for findAndModify in the CRUD spec (since it does refer to behavior of unacknowledged write concerns). findAndModify support for {{hint}} requires 4.4+ (see: SERVER-42099, SERVER-44367). Although findAndModify always returns a response, an unacknowledged write concern seems to force an {{{ok:1}}} response, which hides any error if {{hint}} is unsupported or invalid (e.g. no such index). With respect to this issue, we need only concern ourselves with whether {{hint}} is supported, so raising an error if {{hint}} is used on a pre-4.4 server with an unacknowledged write concern is sufficient.
----
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

This issue was originally reported after starting work on language tickets for DRIVERS-991, which asked drivers to raise client-side errors for any use of {{hint}} with unacknowledged writes. This ticket seeks to revert that change and make an allowance for cases where drivers know that the server does support the {{hint}} option. Since DRIVERS-991 was filed, libmongoc and dependent drivers have chosen not to implement that ticket and are instead waiting on this ticket to be resolved. This ticket has languished for more than a year since and remains outstanding.
h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?

Applications that would like to utilize {{hint}} options with unacknowledged writes.
h4. How does this affect the end user?

The behavior is currently prohibited by our specifications despite being possible to implement. In hindsight, it was premature to add the blanket prohibition.
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

A minority of applications make individual use of {{hint}} and unacknowledged writes. The intersection of applications using both features together is even rarer.
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?

Inability to execute a supported operation.
h4. Is this issue urgent?

No.
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?

No.
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

This ticket affects both driver functionality and our spec tests.
{panel}

summary: isClientError check in unified test runner should unwrap BulkWriteExceptions
description: Per PHPC-1960, not all BulkWriteExceptions are server-side errors despite the class inheriting ServerException. The unified test runner should check whether BulkWriteException is proxying a previous exception before evaluating {{code}}, {{codeName}}, and {{isClientError}} checks in {{MongoDB\Tests\UnifiedSpecTests\ExpectedError::assert()}}.

summary: FindAndModify::isAcknowledgedWriteConcern() fails to detect some acknowledged write concerns
description:
In [ee91ccf|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/commit/ee91ccf486c05b77dda3f8100390671ecf8f7c03] for PHPLIB-539, an internal {{isAcknowledgedWriteConcern}} method was introduced to support error reporting for using a {{hint}} option with a FindAndModify operation (e.g. FindOneAndUpdate). That function currently requires a {{w}} value greater than 1 or a true {{j}} value, but fails to consider {{w=1}} or a string value (e.g. "majority"). A string value will likely satisfy the comparison check, but it'd be preferable to explicitly handle strings here.

This came up while working on DRIVERS-1340, which will entail porting the {{isAcknowledgedWriteConcern}} to the Update, Delete, and BulkWrite operations.

summary: Support $merge and $out executing on secondaries
description:
{panel:title=Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    TBD once scoping is complete.
    {panel}

    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    {panel:title=Epic Summary|borderStyle=dashed|borderColor=#ccc|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}

h4. Summary


h4. Motivation

summary: Allow GridFS StreamWrapper to throw on error instead of emitting a PHP warning
description:
The GridFS StreamWrapper's {{stream_read}} and {{stream_write}} methods currently catch exceptions from our stream classes and instead trigger PHP warnings and return false. While this complies with the suggested [streamWrapper API|https://www.php.net/manual/en/class.streamwrapper.php], it may be more useful to expose users to the original exception instead.

Before making this change, we should see if might cause adverse affects with PHP's own stream functions (e.g. {{stream_get_contents()}}).

Implementing this would allow us to remove some special handling in our GridFS and unified spec test runners that currently check for PHP warnings instead of exceptions to detect certain GridFS errors.

summary: Aggregate allowDiskUse option should be unset by default
description:
The Aggregate operation currently defaults {{allowDiskUse}} to {{false}}, which means it is sent to the server by default.

This conflicts with the [CRUD spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/crud/crud.rst#read], which states that it should be unset by default. Note that the [aggregate command reference|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/aggregate/] also does not specify a server-side default value for the {{allowDiskUse}} option (presumably it is false).

When making this change, we should also revise any PHPLIB documentation that may imply it defaults to false. Likewise for the corresponding Find option introduced by PHPLIB-500 (just docs, as the functionality there is correct).

summary: Ignore read preference for $out/$merge on secondaries if any servers are pre-5.0
description:
This ticket was split from DRIVERS-1969, please see that ticket for a detailed description. 

This issue will be considered a bug fix as the original implementation in PHPLIB-651 introduced a possible BC break whereby a read non-primary preference could be utilized in a mixed-version cluster and result in a server selection error instead of falling back to the primary (expected behavior).

summary: Support authorizedCollections option for listCollections
description: This ticket was split from DRIVERS-1393, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: ModifyCollection refers to undeclared wireVersionForWriteConcern var
description: This dates back to [44f36ce|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/commit/44f36ce366031f2d0505b9d21922a5c87632d51f#diff-6c59c6492e17946f652152edeef2d092aaf39ad276bd5370d68f114253f19831R102] from PHPLIB-66. It will be fixed when the code is deleted as part of PHPLIB-795.

summary: Allow CachingIterator to handle Iterators with non-unique keys
description:
CachingIterator currently caches items in a PHP array using the original keys from the wrapped Traversable. If the Traversable has any duplicate keys, CachingIterator is prone to overwriting cached elements and returning an incorrect count.

We should be able to fix this by changing the cache into a list of key/value tuples.

Note that this is not a problem for MongoDB cursors, since PHPC uses sequential integers for its cursor keys.

summary: Unified test runner should error for unexpected operation arguments
description: This came up in [mongodb/mongo-c-driver#798|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/798#discussion_r640895067]. Since PHPLIB's operations are likely to ignore unsupported options, the test runner would do well to raise an error for unsupported keys are present. We do this in some places elsewhere when parsing test files (e.g. parsing entity definitions), but I don't believe it's done for operation arguments.
